summary: Excellent Springfield show with standout Franklin's Tower and Truckin', captured on a decent audience recording that needs a slight pitch tweak.
review: Reviewers hail this performance as the best of the ’80 tour, highlighting fiery versions of Franklin’s Tower, High Time, Saint of Circumstance, and an ace Truckin' plus a superb Brokedown Palace. The source is an audience (AUD) recording that sounds dry and runs slightly slow, so a modest pitch increase (+1‑2%) is recommended, but overall the audio is clear enough to enjoy the music.

ai_rating.rationale: The show receives glowing praise with many standout songs and only minor performance dents; recording quality is good but requires pitch correction, leading to a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Dry mix that sounds clear but runs slightly slow; listeners recommend increasing pitch by ~1‑2%. The Download 7 version has edited drum cuts to fit on two CDs. Overall the Tim Friend AUD is considered the best source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is long and builds from a warm‑up into several high‑energy jams; the second set delivers strong highlights (High Time, Saint of Circumstance, Truckin') with an experimental Space/Wharf Rat interlude, and the encore closes on a superb Brokedown Palace.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone praised on Mexicali Blues, Franklin's Tower and the solo in Brokedown Palace; he also provides fills to Brent and leads jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work noted as solid with good harmonies on High Time; he trades spots with Jerry during Around.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives kudos for keyboard fills, especially on High Time and the Space section of Saint of Circumstance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers have a brief edited drum break in the Download version; overall rhythm section praised for tightness during Space and Truckin'.
